myth: NoSQL is always faster than SQL
truth: NoSQL trades ACID guarantees for horizontal scalability. For structured data with complex joins, PostgreSQL often outperforms MongoDB. The choice depends on data model and access patterns, not raw speed.

myth: Adding more indexes always speeds up queries
truth: Each index slows down INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE operations because the index must be maintained. Too many indexes waste storage and can confuse the query planner. Profile with EXPLAIN ANALYZE before adding indexes.

myth: 3NF is always the best normalization level
truth: Over-normalization leads to excessive JOINs hurting read performance. Data warehouses intentionally denormalize (star/snowflake schemas) for analytical queries. The right level depends on read/write ratio.
